questions.q: What does a DaemonSet guarantee about pod placement?
options: Exactly one pod runs on the cluster at all times
One pod runs on every node (or every matching node)
Pods are spread evenly across all nodes
The pod is replicated across all namespaces
questions.explanation: A DaemonSet ensures one pod instance runs on every node (or every node matching a nodeSelector/nodeAffinity). New nodes automatically get a pod; removed nodes have the pod deleted.

questions.q: By default, DaemonSet pods do NOT run on control-plane nodes. What must you add to the DaemonSet to make it run on control-plane nodes too?
options: A nodeSelector targeting the master node
A toleration for the control-plane taint
A NodeAffinity with operator Exists for the master label
questions.explanation: Control-plane nodes have the taint `node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule`. Adding a toleration for this taint allows DaemonSet pods to also run on control-plane nodes.

questions.q: Which Kubernetes component manages DaemonSet pods?
questions.explanation: The kube-controller-manager runs the DaemonSet controller, which watches nodes and creates/deletes pods accordingly. This differs from Static Pods, which are managed directly by kubelet.

questions.q: You want to create a DaemonSet quickly using kubectl. What is the recommended approach?
options: Generate a Deployment YAML with --dry-run=client, then edit kind and remove replicas/strategy
kubectl apply -f daemonset.yaml without any generation
kubectl expose deployment to create a DaemonSet
questions.explanation: There is no kubectl create daemonset command. The common approach is to generate a Deployment YAML with --dry-run=client -o yaml, then change kind to DaemonSet and remove the replicas and strategy fields.

questions.q: Deleting a DaemonSet also deletes all the pods it manages.
questions.explanation: True. When you delete a DaemonSet, all its pods are also deleted. You can use --cascade=orphan to delete the DaemonSet without deleting its pods, but this is rarely recommended.
